items.title: Mobile CT scanner visits region where 'at least one smoker in every house'

children.content: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

image.altText: A white road train parked in a parking lot with starts coming out of its side, and shelter. Sign reads 'Heart of Australia'

items.title: Matt Wright sued for negligence after airboat allegedly crashed into tree

children.content: A man who says he suffered a "psychiatric injury" after witnessing injuries sustained by his wife in a 2023 airboat crash is suing tour operator and Netflix star Matt Wright for negligence.

image.altText: A man in a button-up shirt and jeans, walking towards the courthouse while holding his wife's hand.

items.title: NT Labor MLA caught speeding multiple times, years after fatal crash conviction

children.content: Manuel Brown had his licence suspended for three months last October, which was not disclosed publicly at the time. The Arafura MLA also failed to disclose a conviction over a fatal crash when he was initially preselected for Labor in 2023.

gallery.altText: Man in suit sits on chair and puts pen in mouth

thumbnail.altText: Man in suit sits on chair and puts pen in mouth

items.title: Second Mongols bikie to escape custody in the NT caught in eight minutes

children.content: A "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw bikie gang has been arrested after fleeing custody at Royal Darwin Hospital — the second time a Mongols member has fled custody in the Northern Territory this month.

image.altText: The side door of a NT Police car.

items.title: Ex-CEO vindicated in $2.3 million court ruling against space firm

children.content: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

image.altText: A woman with a brown ponytail wearing hi-vis smiles at a worksite

items.title: One CDU employee has been back paid $242k amid $4m stolen wages bill

children.content: Charles Darwin University (CDU) has been directed to back pay more than $4 million in wages after it was found to have underpaid more than 800 professional and academic staff members between 2016 and 2022.

items.title: NT Labor leader defends office culture in 'unique' political workplace

children.content: Northern Territory Opposition Leader Selena Uibo has defended her handling of workplace misconduct allegations in her office, saying it is a "highly-charged political environment that is unique and unlike most workplaces".

image.altText: A woman, looking serious, standing in front of a few media microphones at an outdoor press conference.

items.title: Government responses criticised two months on from Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Eight weeks on from the alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby, Alice Springs community leaders are calling for an audit to determine why federal and territory government spending does not seem to be improving Indigenous living conditions and safety.

image.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

articles.title: Who is going to qualify for the World Cup knockout rounds?

children.content: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

image.altText: Aiden O'Neill (left) and Harry Souttar look on

articles.title: Key Socceroos cog ruled out with injury ahead of crucial Paraguay game

children.content: Wing-back Jacob Italiano is ruled out of Australia's crucial World Cup clash with Paraguay after suffering a groin injury.

image.altText: Jacob Italiano #4 of Australia controls the ball during the FIFA World Cup 2026

children.content: Haiti scores its first World Cup goal in 52 years, as Scotland's hopes of making it to the knockouts teeter against Brazil. Follow live.

items.title: Scotland's World Cup hopes hang by a thread as Haiti makes history

updates.title: FT: Brazil power past Scotland to top group
FT: Morocco beats Haiti 4-2 in thriller

items.title: ASIO reveals new details on AUKUS espionage, warns of 'unprecedented threats'

children.content: Australia's domestic spy chief, Mike Burgess, has defended the agency's resourcing decisions in the years leading up to the Bondi terror attack, arguing his agency is grappling with an "unprecedented" volume of threats.

image.altText: Mike Burgess, Head of ASIO, generic

items.title: As Ebola spreads, Australia faces rise in 'biblical' disease risks

children.content: The bugs that cause Ebola, hantavirus and diphtheria are microscopic, but many of the barriers to controlling them are human-sized.

image.altText: illustration of 4 people in a web of dots representing how viruses spread

items.title: House prices in two Australian capitals tipped to fall substantially

children.content: House price falls of up to 7 and 8 per cent in Sydney and Melbourne have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain. But it is far from doom and gloom for existing owners, with resale profits hitting a record.

image.altText: A sign in front of a building that reads 'Sold'

items.title: Woman fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars

children.content: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

image.altText: A woman with black haair close-up looking sad

items.title: France and Vanuatu clash over remote Pacific island where 'god resides'

children.content: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

items.title: How a discarded coffee cup helped solve a 24-year cold case killing

children.content: Twenty-four years after the killing of a Canberra grandmother, a coffee cup discarded on an east coast road trip leads to the arrest and convictions of her killers.

image.altText: An older man and woman sitting on a bench and smiling.

items.title: ASX set to edge higher ahead of jobs data, and oil slides

children.content: The ASX is set for another small gain after a mixed Wall St session and another retreat in oil prices. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Traffic cones on statues and a sea of orange: The traditions taking over the World Cup

children.content: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

children.content: Think you know your Mad Max from your Muriel's Wedding? Take our Australian film quiz to find out.

children.content: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

items.title: What happened when I embraced the Swedish right to camp anywhere you want

children.content: I've hated camping since I was a teenager, but embracing the spirit of Sweden's Allemansrätten — the right to camp wherever you like — changed everything.

items.title: Former Liberal vice-president Teena McQueen dies after short illness

children.content: Teena McQueen, who served as the federal Liberal Party vice-president from 2017 to 2023, died on Wednesday after a battle with a short but severe medical condition.

image.altText: McQueen smiles holding a microphone while on stage, a TV strap reads 'The Future of the Liberal Party

items.title: Small designers hitting roadblocks trying to get support against Shein copies

children.content: Small Australian fashion designers who have had their designs copied by overseas ultra-fast fashion companies say they have hit continuous roadblocks trying to get support.

image.altText: A composite shows a woman in a blue striped dress next to the Shein website selling the same dress.

items.title: Family of 17yo killed in dog attack want mandatory owner licences

children.content: Annalyse Blyton was killed in a dog attack in the NSW Hunter Valley last year. Her family is pushing for changes to dog ownership laws.

image.altText: A man and woman hold a framed photo of Annalyse Blyton. They are wearing jumpers with a pink heart logo.

items.title: 'Heartbreaking': Sydney boarding house to be turned into luxury flats

children.content: A rundown boarding house which was once a haven for vulnerable people in inner Sydney is set to be converted into luxury apartments.

image.altText: Beige two-storey apartment building in dim, flat lighting

items.title: Knights player Kepaoa banned for six weeks for homophobic slur

children.content: Newcastle Knights player Asu Kepaoa is handed a six-week ban for using a homophobic slur during a NSW Cup match.

image.altText: A footballer looks to break through a tackle.

items.title: Deadly bird flu discovered in second Australian state

children.content: A third confirmed case of the deadly H5 bird flu has been found in a migratory bird on Australia's southern coast.

image.altText: Warnings a much deadlier strain of avian influenza could arrive this spring

items.title: The alternative burial method that could soon come to Australia

children.content: Advocates say human composting offers a sustainable, greener alternative to burials and crematoriums, and can also provide a cheaper and equitable option after death.

gallery.altText: A human compost vessel at recompose facility in Seattle.

thumbnail.altText: A human compost vessel at recompose facility in Seattle.

items.title: France reports first Ebola case in doctor returning from DRC

children.content: France has confirmed its first case of Ebola, a doctor who had flown back from the Democratic Republic of Congo.

image.altText: A nurse slips on protective gloves as she gets into her PPE in a hospital.

items.title: Award-winning artist accused of copying second artwork

children.content: Australian artist Jane Allan, revealed to have previously copied another painter's work, is being accused of a second case of imitation.

image.altText: An artwork featuring a human-like outline.

items.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free after more than two centuries

children.content: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.

thumbnail.altText: A dark coloured feral pig in bushland

items.title: Police to review case of Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre

children.content: The interactions Jeffery Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre had with Australian police before her death will be reviewed, WA's police commissioner confirms.

image.altText: woman with white blonde hair and wearing a navy suit looks down the camera

items.title: Has One Nation won the 'family feud' of Australian conservatives?

children.content: There's a "family feud" in Australia's political right, and right now it appears One Nation is winning.

image.altText: A woman in a red, with red hair, standing in a corridor in front of TV cameras.

items.title: Showers to chase away the chill of Queensland's winter

children.content: A warm change will arrive in Queensland this weekend, along with scattered rain and warnings of strong winds in the north.

image.altText: Brisbane skyline on a rainy day

items.title: The public can soon see this album hidden away for 126 years

children.content: The Royal Society of Tasmania has owned an album of Owen Stanley's maritime artworks since 1900, but its delicate nature meant it could not be put on display. Now, the society is converting the album into a digital flip book so everyone can see it.

image.altText: A watercolour painting of a boat at a jetty, People watch on from the shore.

items.title: Bird flu has arrived in Australia

children.content: After years of waiting, the deadly H5N1 strain of bird flu has finally arrived in Australia.
Two birds found on the southern coast of Western Australia near Esperance have been confirmed as infected, but there is no evidence so far of a mass-mortality event.
So, what actually is H5N1, and why does it matter?
